CREATE TABLE TestFloat (id INT, value FLOAT);
INSERT INTO TestFloat (id, value) VALUES (1, 1.0);
INSERT INTO TestFloat (id, value) VALUES (2, -1.0);
INSERT INTO TestFloat (id, value) VALUES (3, 0.0);
INSERT INTO TestFloat (id, value) VALUES (4, 3.4028235E38);
INSERT INTO TestFloat (id, value) VALUES (5, -3.4028235E38);
INSERT INTO TestFloat (id, value) VALUES (6, NULL);
SELECT * FROM TestFloat WHERE value > 0.0;
SELECT * FROM TestFloat WHERE value < -2.0;
SELECT id, value FROM TestFloat WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT AVG(value) FROM TestFloat WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM TestFloat WHERE value BETWEEN -1.0 AND 1.0;
CREATE TABLE AdditionalFloat (id INT, ref_id INT, float_value FLOAT, FOREIGN KEY(ref_id) REFERENCES TestFloat(id));
INSERT INTO AdditionalFloat (id, ref_id, float_value) VALUES (1, 1, 2.5);
INSERT INTO AdditionalFloat (id, ref_id, float_value) VALUES (2, 2, 4.5);
SELECT T.id, T.value, A.float_value FROM TestFloat T JOIN AdditionalFloat A ON T.id = A.ref_id WHERE A.float_value > 2.0;
SELECT T.id, SUM(A.float_value) as total_value FROM TestFloat T LEFT JOIN AdditionalFloat A ON T.id = A.ref_id GROUP BY T.id HAVING total_value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT T.id FROM TestFloat T WHERE EXISTS (SELECT * FROM AdditionalFloat A WHERE A.ref_id = T.id AND A.float_value < -2.0);
SELECT T.id FROM TestFloat T WHERE value = (SELECT MAX(value) FROM TestFloat);
UPDATE TestFloat SET value = NULL WHERE id = 3;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM TestFloat WHERE value IS NULL;
DELETE FROM TestFloat WHERE value < 0.0;
